finalized_plan.description: Review the USGS NAS database homepage and query interface to identify the available filters (species, geography, date range, nonindigenous status) and locate any documentation or help pages related to taxonomic criteria

Identify the specific scientific names or identifiers used in the database that represent “nonindigenous crocodiles,” ensuring that native species (like the American alligator) are excluded, and gather details on the query parameter for the nonindigenous flag
Check the documentation or help section for confirmation details on the taxonomic criteria used to designate species as nonindigenous, so that this metadata can be included in the final answer

Formulate the precise query parameters using the filters for the state of Florida, the date range (2000–2020), and the accepted nonindigenous crocodile species identifiers. Confirm that these filters correctly exclude native species
Execute the query (via the NAS database interface) to retrieve and count the matching records, and collect any additional metadata or confirmation details regarding the taxonomic criteria used
Compile the results into a final answer with a short summary, an extremely detailed explanation that includes the query process and confirmation details, and any additional relevant context

root_cause_reasoning: The agent has already found the USGS website "https://nas.er.usgs.gov/", and the next step should be to visit that webpage.
notes: Without knowledge of the ground-truth URL, the evaluator cannot verify whether the search tool returns accurate results.
